title: Pebbles and the Autumn Treasure
author: Inspired by J. A. Puddephat
credits: Original character by J. A. Puddephat
Story and image descriptions by Gemini

right.content:
The leaves on the big oak tree were turning the color of a rusty old kettle. Pebbles knew what that meant—it was nearly time for the Big Sleep.

His five big brothers and five little sisters were very busy. They were stuffing the nest with so much moss and dry grass that Pebbles could hardly find a place to sit! 

“I’m going to find the best treasure in the woods for our winter bed,” Pebbles told his Dad.

left.content:
“That’s a grand idea,” said Dad, twitching his whiskers. “But remember, the wind is getting chilly. Don’t stay out too long, or you’ll start yawning before you get home!”

Pebbles scampered down the trunk. He didn't like being on the ground, but the moss felt like a thick, green carpet under his paws. 

He was looking for something special. Something soft. Something... golden.

right.content:
Suddenly, he spotted it! Stuck in a prickly bramble was a long, snowy-white feather. 

It was an owl feather—owls are big birds that fly silently at night, and they have the softest feathers in the world to help them sneak up on things. 

“This will keep Mum and Dad so warm,” Pebbles whispered. He reached out to grab it, but a gust of wind blew it right over the hedge!

left.content:
“Hey! Stop that feather!” Pebbles cried. He chased it past the hazel bushes and over the fallen logs. 

He was running so fast he didn't see the big, bushy tail blocking his path. 

*Oof!* 

Pebbles tumbled head-over-tail and landed right in a pile of crunchy, brown leaves.

right.content:
“Looking for this?” said a chattering voice. 

It was Sammy the Squirrel. He was holding the white feather in his paws. 

“Oh, please,” said Pebbles, catching his breath. “It’s for my family’s winter nest. We have eleven children, you see, so it gets very crowded and we need to stay cozy.”

left.content:
Sammy tapped his chin. “I tell you what. I’ll give you the feather if you help me find my hidden stash of acorns. I’ve buried so many I’ve forgotten where the best ones are!” 

“I’m very good at finding things,” said Pebbles bravely. 

They searched under the ferns and behind the old stone wall. Pebbles used his sensitive nose to sniff the earth.

right.content:
“There!” shouted Pebbles. Under a giant yellow sycamore leaf—sycamore leaves are shaped like a hand with spread-out fingers—lay three big, fat acorns. 

“Hooray!” chattered Sammy. He was so happy he did a little dance. 

“A deal is a deal,” Sammy said, and he tucked the soft white feather into Pebbles’ arms.

left.content:
The journey home was hard because the feather was nearly as big as Pebbles! 

He had to carry it over the reeds and under the holly bush, making sure not to let the white fluff get dirty. 

By the time he reached the old oak tree, the stars were out, and he was feeling very, very sleepy.

right.content:
When he wiggled into the nest, Mum and Dad gasped. 

“A winter blanket!” cried Mum. They spread the feather over all the brothers and sisters. It was the softest, warmest bed in the whole woods. 

“Well done, Pebbles,” whispered Dad. “You really are a grown-up dormouse now.”

left.content:
Pebbles let out a huge yawn. He felt warm, he felt proud, and most of all, he felt sleepy. 

“Goodnight Mama, goodnight Papa,” he murmured. 

And before the moon could move an inch across the sky, Pebbles and his ten brothers and sisters were fast asleep, dreaming of the springtime to come.
